__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics.

description: mdk LLM cost + token volume over the two cost-related instruments mdk.run.tokens and mdk.run.cost_usd. Source: src/movate/tracing/metrics.py. Prescriptive layer: triage flow + per-chart notes + drill-down links. Both counters carry only the 'tenant' attribute; per-agent/per-provider breakdown lives on agent.execute span attributes -- query Jaeger/Langfuse to slice that way.

options.content:
## mdk - cost (tokens + USD)

**What this shows.** Per-tenant LLM spend (USD) and token volume from `mdk.run.cost_usd` and `mdk.run.tokens`. Both counters carry only the `tenant` attribute; **per-agent/per-provider splits live on the `agent.execute` span** (query Jaeger / Langfuse), not on these metrics.

**Normal range.** Cost is observed, not paged - there is no SLO alert rule for cost. Healthy steady-state: USD/hr and tok/s proportional to throughput; **USD per 1k tokens stable**. A 2x step-change in any of these without a workload change is the triage trigger.

**Triage flow.**

```mermaid
graph TD
  A[Cost anomaly noticed] --> B[Pick tenant in dropdown]
  B --> C{Tokens spiked?}
  C -- yes --> C1{Input or output dominant?}
  C1 -- input --> C2[Look for KB chunk-size regression / retrieval over-fetch]
  C1 -- output --> C3[Prompt/temperature drift; mdk eval on the agent]
  C -- no  --> D{USD/1k tokens spiked?}
  D -- yes --> D1[Provider price change OR model swap; check agent.execute provider/model]
  D -- no  --> E[Mix shift -> expensive agent's share grew; Langfuse session view]
```

List fallback:
1. Tokens up? -> input vs output -> KB regression or prompt drift.
2. USD/1k tokens up? -> provider/model change.
3. Otherwise: workload mix shift; Langfuse.

**Cross-surface.** KQL equivalent in `infra/azure-monitor/workbooks/platform.workbook.json` (cost section).

panels.description: Cumulative LLM cost in USD for the selected tenant over the dashboard window.
panels.title: Total spend (window)

panels.description: USD-per-second burn rate for the selected tenant. Multiplied by 3600 gives USD/hr.
panels.title: Spend rate by tenant

options.content:
**What this measures.** Cumulative USD over the dashboard window and the live spend rate by tenant. Source: `mdk.run.cost_usd`.

**Normal.** Proportional to throughput, flat USD/hr line. No SLO alert (cost is observed only; see ADR 017 budget posture).

**If you see X.**

- `step-change in USD/hr with throughput flat` -> model swap or provider price change. Open Jaeger and group `agent.execute` traces by the `provider` / `model_override` attributes.
- `cost climbing while throughput flat` -> per-run cost climbing; pair with the tokens dashboard below. If input tokens up -> KB chunk-size regression; if output -> prompt drift; run `mdk eval` for the affected agent.
- `cost going to zero` -> usage telemetry broken; verify `record_run_usage` in `src/movate/tracing/metrics.py` is being called and `MOVATE_TRACE_SINK` is `otlp` or `both`.

**ADR refs.** ADR 017 (cost) / ADR 015 (Langfuse drilldown). **Alert rule.** None today.

panels.description: Tokens consumed per second by tenant. Counter is total (input + output) -- the OTel instrument does not break direction out as an attribute today, see src/movate/tracing/metrics.py record_run_usage().

links.title: Jaeger - agent.execute (token deltas in span attrs)

panels.title: Tokens/sec by tenant

panels.description: USD per 1k tokens, derived = spend / (tokens / 1000). Useful for spotting drift in average model cost as the workload mix changes.
panels.title: Effective USD per 1k tokens

options.content:
**What this measures.** Tokens/sec by tenant (`mdk.run.tokens` is the combined input+output total - **direction is not broken out today**; per-direction splits live on the `agent.execute` span attributes) and the derived USD-per-1k-tokens ratio.

**Normal.** USD/1k stable to ~1 decimal place; tokens/sec proportional to throughput.

**If you see X.**

- `tokens/sec doubles, throughput flat` -> per-run token use rising. Drill via Jaeger; look at `kb_search` children of `agent.execute` for input swell (KB chunk-size regression), or compare prompt/response sizes in Langfuse for output swell.
- `USD/1k tokens steps up` -> model swap or provider price change; verify the `provider`/`model_override` attributes on recent `agent.execute` spans (don't trust dashboards for that; this metric doesn't carry it).
- `tokens flat, USD climbing` -> almost always a billing-side reclassification by the provider; check `mdk eval` cost reports.

**ADR refs.** ADR 015 / ADR 017. **Alert rule.** None today.

title: mdk - cost (tokens + USD)
